nodes.x: And Job answered and said,
But Job answered and said,
Then Job answered and said,
Then Job answered the Lord, and said,
¶ Then Job answered the Lord, and said,
And Job spake, and said,
No doubt but ye are the people, and wisdom shall die with you.
The lips of the righteous feed many: but fools die for want of wisdom.
And behold, I tell you these things that ye may learn wisdom; that ye may learn that when ye are in the service of your fellow beings ye are only in the service of your God.
Give me now wisdom and knowledge, that I may go out and come in before this people: for who can judge this thy people, that is so great?

We see then to whom the word of wisdom belongs, and we believe it right to teach it in connexion with every other principle necessary for the perfection of the people of God ; while, on the other hand, an adherence to the word of wisdom in the neglect of the other commandments of the Lord, is like faith without works — it is dead, being alone, and will profit us nothing.
And their wisdom shall be great, and their understanding reach to heaven; and before them the wisdom of the wise shall perish, and the understanding of the prudent shall come to naught.
But I have understanding as well as you; I am not inferior to you: yea, who knoweth not such things as these?
What ye know, the same do I know also: I am not inferior unto you.
Ye have not applied your hearts to understanding; therefore, ye have not been wise. Therefore, what teach ye this people?
I perceive that ye are weak, that ye cannot understand all my words which I am commanded of the Father to speak unto you at this time.
Have not I written to thee excellent things in counsels and knowledge,
Yea, if thou criest after knowledge, and liftest up thy voice for understanding;
I am as one mocked of his neighbour, who calleth upon God, and he answereth him: the just upright man is laughed to scorn.
He that sitteth in the heavens shall laugh: the Lord shall have them in derision.
Be not deceived; God is not mocked: for whatsoever a man soweth, that shall he also reap.
He that despiseth his neighbour sinneth: but he that hath mercy on the poor, happy is he.
Fools make a mock at sin: but among the righteous there is favour.

At this I put forth my hand and caught him by the trunk, and
lifting him from the ground by the power of the Spirit, I dashed
him to the ground a number of times; at this he seemed to dwindle
down to about the size of a suckling calf, and finally turned into
a serpent and swelled out to the length of about a hundred feet,
and half the size of a man's body. He then spoke great swelling
words in…
He that is ready to slip with his feet is as a lamp despised in the thought of him that is at ease.
He that sleeps when the sun shines, and lights his lamp when it does not, despises the lamp of the Lord, and taxes his eyes and purse for nought.
He maketh my feet like hinds’ feet: and setteth me upon my high places.
He maketh my feet like hinds’ feet, and setteth me upon my high places.
Be not hasty to go out of his sight: stand not in an evil thing; for he doeth whatsoever pleaseth him.
The tabernacles of robbers prosper, and they that provoke God are secure; into whose hand God bringeth abundantly.
¶ And they brought the tabernacle unto Moses, the tent, and all his furniture, his taches, his boards, his bars, and his pillars, and his sockets,
If iniquity be in thine hand, put it far away, and let not wickedness dwell in thy tabernacles.
Who found favour before God, and desired to find a tabernacle for the God of Jacob.
And they shall keep thy charge, and the charge of all the tabernacle: only they shall not come nigh the vessels of the sanctuary and the altar, that neither they, nor ye also, die.
My tabernacle also shall be with them: yea, I will be their God, and they shall be my people.
But ask now the beasts, and they shall teach thee; and the fowls of the air, and they shall tell thee:
And it shall come to pass that the beasts of the forest and the fowls of the air shall devour them up.
Of fowls after their kind, and of cattle after their kind, of every creeping thing of the earth after his kind, two of every sort shall come unto thee, to keep them alive.

Then will I leave thee upon the land, I will cast thee forth upon the open field, and will cause all the fowls of the heaven to remain upon thee, and I will fill the beasts of the whole earth with thee.
Shall not they teach thee, and tell thee, and utter words out of their heart?
They shall be left together unto the fowls of the mountains, and to the beasts of the earth: and the fowls shall summer upon them, and all the beasts of the earth shall winter upon them.
Or speak to the earth, and it shall teach thee: and the fishes of the sea shall declare unto thee.
Therefore shall the land mourn, and every one that dwelleth therein shall languish, with the beasts of the field, and with the fowls of heaven; yea, the fishes of the sea also shall be taken away.
Neither is it beyond the sea, that thou shouldest say, Who shall go over the sea for us, and bring it unto us, that we may hear it, and do it?
And I give unto you a commandment that then ye shall teach them unto all men; for they shall be taught unto all nations, kindreds, tongues and people.
And now, Moses, my son, I will speak unto thee concerning this earth upon which thou standest; and thou shalt write the things which I shall speak.
Who knoweth not in all these that the hand of the Lord hath wrought this?
Behold, the Lord hath a mighty and strong one, which as a tempest of hail and a destroying storm, as a flood of mighty waters overflowing, shall cast down to the earth with the hand.
The Lord hath made bare his holy arm in the eyes of all the nations, and all the ends of the earth shall see the salvation of our God?
And the Lord hath done to him, as he spake by me: for the Lord hath rent the kingdom out of thine hand, and given it to thy neighbour, even to David:
The Lord hath made bare his holy arm in the eyes of all the nations; and all the ends of the earth shall see the salvation of our God.
The Lord hath made bare his holy arm in the eyes of all the nations; and all the ends of the earth shall see the salvation of God.
In whose hand is the soul of every living thing, and the breath of all mankind.
Thou openest thine hand, and satisfiest the desire of every living thing.
And the spirit and the body are the soul of man.
And this according to the mind and will of the Lord, who ruleth over all flesh.
My soul, wait thou only upon God; for my expectation is from him.
And the Lord God formed man of the dust of the ground, and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life; and man became a living soul.
Doth not the ear try words? and the mouth taste his meat?
For the ear trieth words, as the mouth tasteth meat.
Hear attentively the noise of his voice, and the sound that goeth out of his mouth.
They have ears, but they hear not; neither is there any breath in their mouths.
They have mouths, but they speak not; eyes have they, but they see not;
With the ancient is wisdom; and in length of days understanding.
Whence then cometh wisdom? and where is the place of understanding?
For the Lord giveth wisdom: out of his mouth cometh knowledge and understanding.
But where shall wisdom be found? and where is the place of understanding?
I understand more than the ancients, because I keep thy precepts.
With him is wisdom and strength, he hath counsel and understanding.
He that getteth wisdom loveth his own soul: he that keepeth understanding shall find good.
Counsel in the heart of man is like deep water; but a man of understanding will draw it out.
Counsel is mine, and sound wisdom: I am understanding; I have strength.
He that hath knowledge spareth his words: and a man of understanding is of an excellent spirit.
Now, behold this is wisdom; whoso readeth, let him understand and receive also;
Behold, he breaketh down, and it cannot be built again: he shutteth up a man, and there can be no opening.
Then said the Lord unto me; This gate shall be shut, it shall not be opened, and no man shall enter in by it; because the Lord, the God of Israel, hath entered in by it, therefore it shall be shut.

But he that heareth, and doeth not, is like a man that without a foundation built an house upon the earth; against which the stream did beat vehemently, and immediately it fell; and the ruin of that house was great.
Saying, This man began to build, and was not able to finish.
For every one that asketh, receiveth; and he that seeketh, findeth; and to him that knocketh, it shall be opened.
Thou hast broken down all his hedges; thou hast brought his strong holds to ruin.
Behold, he withholdeth the waters, and they dry up: also he sendeth them out, and they overturn the earth.
And he spake unto them concerning their rebellions upon the waters, and the mercies of God in sparing their lives, that they were not swallowed up in the sea.
He sendeth out his word, and melteth them: he causeth his wind to blow, and the waters flow.
And behold, also, if he say unto the waters of the great deep—Be thou dried up—it is done.

¶ Though he be fruitful among his brethren, an east wind shall come, the wind of the Lord shall come up from the wilderness, and his spring shall become dry, and his fountain shall be dried up: he shall spoil the treasure of all pleasant vessels.
Who giveth rain upon the earth, and sendeth waters upon the fields:
With him is strength and wisdom: the deceived and the deceiver are his.
Let no man deceive himself. If any man among you seemeth to be wise in this world, let him become a fool, that he may be wise.
And the works of truth and the works of deceit are written upon the hearts of men, and each one of them the Lord knoweth.
With him is wisdom and strength, he hath counsel and understanding.
And thus are the secrets of his heart made manifest; and so falling down on his face he will worship God, and report that God is in you of a truth.
He leadeth counsellors away spoiled, and maketh the judges fools.
And again, he has reviled against our lawyers, and our judges.
¶ Nevertheless the Lord raised up judges, which delivered them out of the hand of those that spoiled them.
The way of a fool is right in his own eyes: but he that hearkeneth unto counsel is wise.
Judge not according to the appearance, but judge righteous judgment.
He looseth the bond of kings, and girdeth their loins with a girdle.
And the king of Israel answered and said, Tell him, Let not him that girdeth on his harness boast himself as he that putteth it off.
And righteousness shall be the girdle of his loins, and faithfulness the girdle of his reins.
He leadeth princes away spoiled, and overthroweth the mighty.

And he put upon him the coat, and girded him with the girdle, and clothed him with the robe, and put the ephod upon him, and he girded him with the curious girdle of the ephod, and bound it unto him therewith.
The king sent and loosed him; even the ruler of the people, and let him go free.
He leadeth princes away spoiled, and overthroweth the mighty.
He poureth contempt upon princes, and weakeneth the strength of the mighty.
He shall cut off the spirit of princes: he is terrible to the kings of the earth.
That bringeth the princes to nothing; he maketh the judges of the earth as vanity.
He poureth contempt upon princes, and causeth them to wander in the wilderness, where there is no way.
That he may set him with princes, even with the princes of his people.
He removeth away the speech of the trusty, and taketh away the understanding of the aged.
And he is to take the lead of meetings when there is no elder present;

1. “ Listen,” he said, “concerning the forms 
which you are asking about. 2. Why did she appear 
to you in the first vision as old and seated on a 
chair? Beeause your! spirit is old and already fading 
away, and has no power through your weakness and 
double-mindedness. 3. For just as old people, who 
have no longer any hope of becoming young again,
He poureth contempt upon princes, and weakeneth the strength of the mighty.

And through his policy also he shall cause craft to prosper in his hand; and he shall magnify himself in his heart, and by peace shall destroy many: he shall also stand up against the Prince of princes; but he shall be broken without hand.
He discovereth deep things out of darkness, and bringeth out to light the shadow of death.
He revealeth the deep and secret things: he knoweth what is in the darkness, and the light dwelleth with him.
He setteth an end to darkness, and searcheth out all perfection: the stones of darkness, and the shadow of death.
All darkness shall be hid in his secret places: a fire not blown shall consume him; it shall go ill with him that is left in his tabernacle.

Give glory to the Lord your God, before he cause darkness, and before your feet stumble upon the dark mountains, and, while ye look for light, he turn it into the shadow of death, and make it gross darkness.
He increaseth the nations, and destroyeth them: he enlargeth the nations, and straiteneth them again.
And he raiseth up a righteous nation, and destroyeth the nations of the wicked.
And by the weak things of the earth the Lord shall thresh the nations by the power of his Spirit.
Yea, he will bring other nations unto them, and he will give unto them power, and he will take away from them the lands of their possessions, and he will cause them to be scattered and smitten.
And he that overcometh, and keepeth my works unto the end, to him will I give power over the nations:
Howbeit he meaneth not so, neither doth his heart think so; but in his heart it is to destroy and cut off nations not a few.
He taketh away the heart of the chief of the people of the earth, and causeth them to wander in a wilderness where there is no way.

And strangers, the terrible of the nations, have cut him off, and have left him: upon the mountains and in all the valleys his branches are fallen, and his boughs are broken by all the rivers of the land; and all the people of the earth are gone down from his shadow, and have left him.
And when it was day, he departed and went into a desert place: and the people sought him, and came unto him, and stayed him, that he should not depart from them.
That prepareth his heart to seek God, the Lord God of his fathers, though he be not cleansed according to the purification of the sanctuary.
They grope in the dark without light, and he maketh them to stagger like a drunken man.
They meet with darkness in the daytime, and grope in the noonday as in the night.
In the daytime also he led them with a cloud, and all the night with a light of fire.
They change the night into day: the light is short because of darkness.
Therefore he knoweth their works, and he overturneth them in the night, so that they are destroyed.
